title: Remove Duplicates
description:
Given an array of integers, remove duplicate elements and return the array with only unique elements in sorted order.

Implement an efficient solution.

visibleTestCases.explanation: Duplicates (2 and 4) are removed, unique sorted array is [1,2,3,4].
All elements are duplicates, only one '5' remains.

startCode.initialCode:
# --- USER SOLUTION START ---
def removeDuplicates(arr):
    # TODO: Remove duplicates and return sorted unique array
    return []
# --- USER SOLUTION END ---

import sys
def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data: return
    n = int(input_data[0])
    arr = [int(x) for x in input_data[1:n+1]]
    res = removeDuplicates(arr)
    print(" ".join(map(str, res)))
if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

referenceSolution.completeCode:
# --- USER SOLUTION START ---
def removeDuplicates(arr):
    unique = list(set(arr))
    unique.sort()
    return unique
# --- USER SOLUTION END ---

import sys
def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data: return
    n = int(input_data[0])
    arr = [int(x) for x in input_data[1:n+1]]
    res = removeDuplicates(arr)
    print(" ".join(map(str, res)))
if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
